title: Tidewater Renewables Ltd. Completes Planned Outage at Its Renewable Diesel Refinery and Returns to Full Capacity
content: Tidewater Renewables Ltd. has announced the safe completion of a planned seven-day outage at its renewable diesel and renewable hydrogen complex (the "HDRD Complex") in Prince George, British Columbia. This scheduled downtime was necessary to reinstall a repaired component following an equipment anomaly that caused a short, unplanned outage in October 2025. After a temporary repair allowed the facility to operate at an average utilization of approximately 2,250 bbl/d starting November 1, the Corporation commenced the planned outage on December 6, 2025. During this period, the temporary repair was removed, and the rebuilt component was successfully installed. The outage was completed on December 12, 2025, and utilization at the HDRD Complex has since steadily increased, returning to its full design capacity of 3,000 bbl/day.
author: Analysts Account
